summary: Implemented Scanner that reads project directories, parses index.json files, and builds the in-memory data model using fs.FS interface for testability. Includes ScanAll, ScanProject, Invalidate, SortFeatures, wildcard dependency expansion, phase derivation, and health status computation.
keyDecisions: Used dirFSWrapper marker interface to distinguish production os.DirFS from test fstest.MapFS, enabling correct path handling on both Windows and Unix
Used os.DirFS(projectPath) per-project instead of fs.Sub from root, to handle Windows absolute paths correctly
SortFeatures implemented as package-level function per tech design, sorting blocked-first then completion-ascending then alphabetical

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: internal/scanner/scanner.go implements Scanner struct with fs.FS field
NewScanner(cfg *Config) *Scanner creates scanner using os.DirFS for production
ScanAll() map[string]*ProjectData reads all configured projects
ScanProject(id string) (*ProjectData, error) reads a single project
SortFeatures sorts: blocked first, completion % ascending, alphabetical
Skips projects with invalid paths (adds to ProjectData.Warnings)
Skips features with malformed index.json (logs warning)
Derives PhaseInfo from task IDs in each feature
Expands wildcard dependencies (1.x) to individual task IDs
Unit tests using fstest.MapFS: valid project, missing features dir, malformed JSON, wildcard expansion, sort order
Integration tests using temp directory with real index.json fixtures
